summary: A Qwen3-based local-first model candidate trained on verified failed-to-repaired trajectories at their first causal divergence, surrounded by evidence-bound execution and admission systems.

objective.source: verified failed trajectory paired with an independently verified repaired descendant
components: causal preference margin against the rejected continuation

replay.balancing_unit: replayed attention-area proxy with repair-lineage atomicity

adapter.fallback: ordinary LoRA only when installed PEFT lacks use_rslora; fallback must be recorded

purpose.human_goal: Turn one vague unfinished human intention into a finished, verified digital reality without requiring the person to manage agents, tickets, branches, or ordinary mechanical work.
purpose.model_goal: Learn the causal decisions that separate evidenced failures from verified repairs and generalize those decisions to unfamiliar whole-product objectives.
purpose.non_goal: Produce persuasive chat that is not connected to verified execution.

minimum_model_gate: changed adapter tensors from real gradient steps
strict held-out gain or compute-quality Pareto gain against the frozen base and established lane
no specialist or fused-candidate regression on mandatory gates
zero false promotion or completion claims

compute.cpu_role: contract validation, data compilation, bundle freezing, and toy rehearsal; not the canonical 1.7B NF4 training claim
compute.cuda_role: real specialist gradient training, changed-tensor proof, and full-sequence evaluation

claim_boundary: This card identifies the canonical trainable candidate and its executable method. It does not claim that a successful canonical CUDA run, improved adapter, admitted checkpoint, or general intelligence currently exists.
